# ADR Index

_Auto-generated 2026-05-11 • 16 pages_

> Sub-index of all `adr` pages in `wiki/`.
> Generated by `scripts/update_index.py`.

## ADR (16)

- [[wiki/adrs/0001-sqlite-primary-store-for-code-graph|ADR-0001: SQLite is the primary store for lattice-graph]] — Use SQLite at `<workspace>/.graph/code.db` as the primary store for lattice-graph, with a two-table + metadata schema and recursive CTEs for traversal. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0002-explicit-graph-update-lifecycle|ADR-0002: Explicit, hook-surfaced graph update lifecycle (no auto-update, no FS watcher)]] — Graph updates are explicit (slash commands + a SessionStart staleness banner); no auto-update, no filesystem watcher. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-03)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0003-observability-as-category-gate|ADR-0003: Observability as a single category-gate env var (opt-out)]] — Adopt a single opt-out env var `LATTICE_WORKFLOWS_OBSERVABILITY` to gate the entire observability category in lattice-workflows; all observability hooks check the same var and are fail-open. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-04)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0004-filesystem-affects-resolution-v1|ADR-0004: Filesystem-only `affects:` resolution in work items for v1]] — In v1, `affects:` fields in work items are matched by filesystem path prefix only; graph-aware resolution is deferred until lattice-graph ships. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-07)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0005-sourcetree-sole-domain-model|ADR-0005: SourceTree as the sole domain model for lattice-source-parser]] — A single span-bearing intermediate (SourceNode + Reference + Span) is the only domain object the package exports; all consumer outputs are pure projections on top of it. No subclassing by language; symbol kind is a string tag. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-07)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0006-source-parser-sibling-package|ADR-0006: lattice-source-parser ships as a sibling Python package free of storage and transport concerns]] — The parser lives at packages/lattice-source-parser/, depends only on tree-sitter + tree-sitter-language-pack, and has no knowledge of SQLite, MCP, Claude Code plugins, or ${LATTICE_*} env vars; any Python project can depend on it standalone. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-07)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0007-cli-first-code-graph|ADR-0007: CLI-first surface for lattice-graph — `cg` before MCP]] — v1 ships the cg console-script (on lattice-graph-core) plus 3 slash commands that shell to it; the MCP server adapter slips to v1.1 so the library boundary is exercised through a thin testable adapter first. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-07)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0008-single-writer-code-db|ADR-0008: Single-writer `code.db` — only `cg update` writes; consumers open read-only]] — cg update is the only writer to <workspace>/.graph/code.db; all consumers open the database in read-only mode, enforced via SQLite write-lock and exit code 6 (UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS). _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0009-uv-ruff-python-tooling|ADR-0009: uv workspace + ruff as Python tooling standard across `packages/`]] — All Python packages under packages/ are members of a single uv workspace with a root-level pyproject.toml (workspace coordinator, no [project] table) and a single uv.lock; ruff is the lint+format tool with root-only config; pytest is the test runner; Python 3.12 pinned via .python-version. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-07)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0010-lattice-curator-as-fifth-plugin|ADR-0010: lattice-curator as fifth ecosystem plugin]] — Add a fifth lattice-* plugin that curates task-specific knowledge briefs from the wiki and expert rules into Claude Code via a UserPromptSubmit hook, split as a pure Python package + thin Claude Code plugin per the lattice-source-parser precedent. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0011-single-workspace-root|ADR-0011: Single workspace root `<repo>/lattice/`]] — Consolidate all per-repo lattice data under a single `<repo>/lattice/` workspace root — vault at `lattice/wiki/`, work items at `lattice/work/`, machine state at `lattice/.graph/` — superseding the two-root design that was never implemented. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0012-python-bedrock-stack-for-curator|ADR-0012: Python + Bedrock + LangChain/LangGraph stack for the curator]] — Build lattice-curator in Python (3.12) with langchain-aws for the Bedrock client, langgraph for pipeline orchestration, and Pydantic for structured output. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0013-plans-and-specs-in-lattice-workspace|ADR-0013: Brainstorming specs and plans live under `<workspace>/{specs,plans}/`]] — Relocate `lattice-workflows` brainstorming output and plan output from `docs/lattice-workflows/{specs,plans}/` to `<workspace>/specs/` and `<workspace>/plans/`. Filenames carry a package/plugin prefix inferred from cwd + affected paths, and missing `.lattice.yaml` hard-fails instead of silently writing. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0014-per-plugin-version-tracking-in-lattice-yaml|ADR-0014: Per-plugin version tracking in `.lattice.yaml`]] — Bump `.lattice.yaml` to v2 to record `installed_version` and `applied_version` per plugin; expose `warn_if_stale` / `pending_updates` on `lattice-workspace`; require `version=` on `init()`; coerce v1 lazily; accept PyYAML as a runtime dep on the foundational package. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0015-workspace-root-wikilink-form|ADR-0015: Workspace-root-relative wikilink form]] — Wikilinks in the lattice vault are workspace-root-relative — `[[work/<slug>]]` and `[[wiki/<category>/...]]` are canonical; `[[../work/...]]` and bare `[[packages/...]]` are forbidden. Follows from ADR-0011 placing the Obsidian vault at `<workspace>/`. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-09)_
- [[wiki/adrs/0016-track-pcvelz-superpowers-fork|ADR-0016: Track pcvelz/superpowers rather than obra/superpowers upstream]] — lattice-workflows tracks pcvelz/superpowers as its upstream rather than obra/superpowers. The fork's Claude-Code-native task substrate (TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskList, blockedBy, .tasks.json cross-session resume), plan-mode hard-gate, and opt-in pre-commit/stop-deflection hooks are worth the cost of cross-CLI portability loss, worktree-detection regression, and non-trivial merge-back to obra/. _(accepted · upd 2026-05-10)_
